question: What thermodynamic cycle does the NuScale plant use for power conversion?

nuscale_ch01_0439.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.2 Maintain indirect cycle PWR water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0439.summary: The section outlines NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, as required by GDC 14. It notes that the NuScale steam generator design places primary water outside the tubes and secondary water inside, with no steam generator blowdown, resulting in secondary chemistry requirements that differ from standard guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0029.title: Power Conversion System
nuscale_ch01_0029.summary: The Power Conversion System in the NuScale plant includes a main steam system, turbine generator, condenser, cooling tower, and condensate/feedwater system, allowing flexible operation of multiple NPMs for grid demand and maintenance. The plant also features dedicated systems for fuel handling, storage, and various cooling needs, including closed-loop and open-loop systems to support safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch01_0438.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.1 Maintain direct cycle BWR plant water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0438.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for systems related to condensate cleanup and associated components. It notes that certain requirements are not applicable to NuScale’s design (e.g., those specific to BWRs or Class 2/3 components), while for applicable criteria, such as maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality, NuScale’s design conforms but with differences in secondary chemistry requirements due to its unique steam generator configuration.

nuscale_ch01_0030.title: Fuel Handling and Storage Systems
nuscale_ch01_0030.summary: The fuel handling and storage systems are located in the west end of the reactor building and include areas for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock. These facilities support safe storage and handling of nuclear fuel during refueling and maintenance operations.

nuscale_ch01_0415.title: SRP 10.2, Rev 3: Turbine GeneratorII.1 Protect SSC important to safety from the effects of turbine missiles with a turbine overspeed protection system (GDC 4)
nuscale_ch01_0415.summary: The NuScale plant design does not include or require safety-related emergency diesel generators, and therefore the associated systems (fuel oil storage, cooling water, starting, lubrication, and air intake/exhaust) and their regulatory criteria are not applicable. The plant’s safety functions do not rely on AC or DC power, making these SRP sections irrelevant to NuScale’s design.

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water for pressurized water reactors. While NuScale's design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as a containment spray system or sump), the intent of the guidelines is still met due to the collection and recirculation of ECCS water within the containment vessel. Some criteria, such as those related to minimum containment pressure and dual containment leakage, are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0046.title: Turbine Generator Building
nuscale_ch01_0046.summary: The Turbine Generator Buildings (TGBs) in a NuScale Power Plant are nonsafety-related structures, each housing six turbine generator sets, their auxiliaries, condensers, condensate systems, and feedwater systems. These buildings also include laydown areas and overhead cranes to facilitate installation and maintenance activities.

nuscale_ch01_0186.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.1 Minimum pH for Emergency Coolant Water
nuscale_ch01_0186.summary: This section discusses NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the minimum pH requirements for emergency coolant water in pressurized water reactors. While certain aspects of the referenced guidance (BTP 6-1) do not directly apply due to differences in NuScale’s design—such as the absence of a containment spray system or sump—the intent of the pH acceptance criterion remains applicable because emergency coolant water is still collected and recirculated within the containment vessel.

nuscale_ch01_0705.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.A Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0705.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various transient events affecting the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure. The NuScale design generally conforms to the requirements, except for partial conformance regarding the steam pressure regulator, as the design does not include this component. Analytical parameters and objectives for these events are addressed, with no significant deviations noted except where the design differs from conventional systems.

nuscale_ch01_0710.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.F Performance of Nonsafety-Related Systems and Single Failures of Active and Passive Systems
nuscale_ch01_0710.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), although the design does not include a steam pressure regulator. It states that the performance of nonsafety-related systems and single failures of both active and passive systems conform to requirements, with no exceptions noted. The analytical model used for these evaluations also conforms to the standards.

nuscale_ch01_0447.title: SRP 10.4.8, Rev 3: Steam Generator Blowdown SystemAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0447.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various systems, noting that the NuScale steam generator design does not use a blowdown system, making SRP 10.4.8 not applicable. It also highlights that certain criteria and guidance intended for large LWRs are not relevant to NuScale’s design, and that other systems, such as the auxiliary feedwater system, are replaced by functionally different systems in NuScale’s plant.

nuscale_ch01_0441.title: DSRS 10.4.7, Rev 0: Condensate and Feedwater SystemII.2 Fluid Instabilities (GDC 4)
nuscale_ch01_0441.summary: The section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Condensate and Feedwater System, noting partial compliance with fluid instability requirements (GDC 4) due to differences from large LWR designs. Several criteria, including heat removal capability, inspection, and testing, are deemed not applicable because the system does not transfer heat to an ultimate heat sink. Additionally, NuScale’s design does not use a steam generator blowdown or auxiliary feedwater system, with the DHRS serving a similar but distinct function.
